name: Thermohaline circulation (AMOC)
signal_1: European crop yield divergence from baseline
signal_4: UK/NW Europe heating and cooling demand shifts
asset_classes: Sovereign bonds (UK, NL, IE), RE in coastal NW Europe, agri futures
commodities: European wheat & barley|North Sea cod|Heating energy (NW Europe)|Sovereign bonds (UK, NL, Scandinavia)|Reinsurance
trade_directions: Long: grain futures (lower EU yields)|Long: energy heating demand NW Europe|Short: European coastal real estate debt|Long: reinsurance & CAT bonds
commodity_signal: Would cool NW Europe by 3–8°C, devastate UK/Irish agriculture, shift Gulf Stream fisheries. Single largest systemic tail risk to European commodity markets.

name: Arctic sea ice loss
signal_1: Northern Sea Route transit volumes
asset_classes: Arctic shipping equities, O&G Arctic licences, re-insurance books
commodities: Shipping freight rates|LNG/oil (Arctic routes)|Salmon & pollock|Insurance derivatives|Cold-weather tourism
trade_directions: Long: Arctic shipping ETFs, LNG spot|Short: traditional polar tourism operators|Long: Arctic fishery quotas (new grounds)
commodity_signal: New polar shipping lanes cut Asia–Europe transit ~30%. Opens hydrocarbon drilling. Pollock grounds shift north — watch Norwegian & Alaskan quota allocations.

name: Greenland ice sheet collapse
signal_1: Mass balance anomaly rate
signal_2: Sea level rise acceleration at tide gauges
horizon: Centuries but tipping threshold proximity matters now
asset_classes: Coastal RE globally, municipal bonds in low-lying cities, P&C re-insurance
commodities: Coastal real estate debt|Sovereign bonds (low-lying nations)|CAT bonds & flood reinsurance|Global crop insurance|Port infrastructure bonds
trade_directions: Short: coastal municipal bonds (Miami, Amsterdam, Shanghai)|Long: flood barrier construction|Long: elevated-land real estate|Short: crop insurance near sea-level deltas
commodity_signal: 3–7m sea-level rise over centuries, but non-linear. Sub-century commitments already locked in at current warming. Reinsurers pricing in 50yr horizon flood risk now.

name: West Antarctic ice sheet collapse
signal_1: Thwaites glacier grounding line retreat rate
asset_classes: Low-elevation coastal RE, sovereign debt (small island states), infrastructure bonds
commodities: Same as Greenland but additive|Infrastructure bonds|Agricultural delta land|Port logistics pricing
trade_directions: Short: deltaic agricultural land (Mekong, Ganges, Nile)|Long: Dutch/Israeli water engineering firms|Long: inland freight infrastructure
commodity_signal: Thwaites Glacier alone represents ~0.5m SLR. WAIS collapse and Greenland are partially correlated — combined exposure for coastal infrastructure is the key risk.

name: Coral transitions
signal_1: Bleaching event frequency/severity (NOAA Coral Reef Watch)
signal_4: Coastal protection value erosion (storm buffer loss)
asset_classes: Tourism equities (Caribbean, GBR), coastal property in reef-protected areas, fisheries bonds
commodities: Reef fish (grouper, snapper)|Dive/marine tourism|Coastal reinsurance|Aquaculture feed (fishmeal)
trade_directions: Short: reef-dependent tourism operators|Long: aquaculture (land-based fish farms)|Long: CAT bonds covering coral-dependent coasts
commodity_signal: ~$375B/yr reef tourism at risk. Caribbean and Indo-Pacific most exposed. Fisheries dependent on reef nursery habitat collapse faster than open-ocean stocks.

name: Kelp transitions
signal_1: Kelp canopy extent from satellite (Landsat/Sentinel)
asset_classes: Aquaculture equities, blue carbon credits, fisheries quota values
commodities: Kelp as feed additive (methane reduction)|Abalone & urchin fisheries|Alginate industry|Seaweed biofuel|Rock lobster (kelp-dependent)
trade_directions: Long: kelp aquaculture operators (temp range expansion in some zones)|Short: urchin processing (barrens expansion in warming zones)|Long: seaweed-based feed additive companies (ag methane regulation)
commodity_signal: Asparagopsis (kelp-family) reduces cattle methane by 80%. As methane regulation tightens, kelp-based feed additives become a significant agricultural commodity. Watch Australian urchin barrens.

name: Marine food webs
signal_1: Forage fish biomass indices (anchovy, herring, capelin)
signal_2: Seabird and marine mammal population trends
horizon: Ongoing — accelerating with warming and acidification
asset_classes: Fishmeal/fish oil futures, integrated seafood companies, aquaculture feed producers
commodities: Tuna, swordfish (apex predators)|Fishmeal|Krill-based aquaculture|Omega-3 supply chain
trade_directions: Long: algae-based omega-3 (bypasses fish food web)|Short: tuna-dependent canneries|Long: aquaculture vertically integrated on microalgae
commodity_signal: Jellyfish blooms (food web disruption indicator) are intensifying. Trophic cascade collapses in Black Sea (1980s) and NW Pacific show how fast this can restructure commercial fisheries.

name: Fisheries collapse
signal_1: Stock assessment biomass vs MSY
signal_4: Community food security indices in fishing-dependent economies
detectability_note: annual stock assessments — but lags common
asset_classes: Seafood equities, fisheries quota assets, sovereign risk in fishing-dependent states
commodities: Fishmeal & fish oil|Omega-3 supplements|Aquaculture feed|Soy (substitute protein in feed)|Wild-catch seafood futures
trade_directions: Long: soy meal, insect protein companies|Long: aquaculture equipment & feed tech|Short: wild-catch dependent processors (tuna, anchovy)|Long: fishmeal spot prices (scarcity premium)
commodity_signal: Fishmeal is the hidden driver — collapse of anchovy/capelin raises costs across salmon & shrimp aquaculture. Watch Peru anchovy quota as a leading indicator.

name: Marine eutrophication
signal_1: Harmful algal bloom (HAB) frequency and extent
signal_3: Dissolved oxygen profiles in coastal zones
signal_4: Fertiliser run-off / nitrogen load from catchments
asset_classes: Coastal tourism RE, water utility bonds, fertiliser producers, aquaculture
commodities: Coastal fisheries|Fertiliser regulation|Shrimp & oyster aquaculture|Nitrogen trading schemes|Tourism (coastal amenity)
trade_directions: Short: inshore fishing licences in fertiliser-runoff zones|Long: offshore/deep-water fisheries (less hypoxia exposure)|Long: low-input 'regenerative' agriculture input suppliers
commodity_signal: Gulf of Mexico dead zone driven by Mississippi nitrate load. Shrimp season length is a direct proxy. Track USDA nitrogen application data as a leading indicator for coastal fishery pressure.

name: Hypoxia
signal_1: Dead zone area (satellite + ship surveys)
horizon: Ongoing — Gulf of Mexico, Baltic, Black Sea already persistent
asset_classes: Fisheries in affected regions, waterfront RE, agri-chemical sector
commodities: Bottom-trawl fisheries|Fertiliser input costs|Shrimp|Blue crab|Dead-zone affected grain exports
trade_directions: Long: pelagic (surface) fisheries vs demersal|Short: fertiliser-dependent coastal shrimp operations|Long: water quality monitoring tech
commodity_signal: 200+ coastal dead zones globally. Chesapeake blue crab and Gulf shrimp are directly affected. Iowa/Illinois corn fertiliser applications are a 6-month leading indicator for Gulf hypoxia extent.

name: Seagrass transitions
signal_1: Seagrass meadow extent change (satellite + aerial)
signal_3: Nursery habitat loss for commercial fish species
asset_classes: Blue carbon credits, coastal fisheries, marine tourism, coastal RE
commodities: Juvenile fish habitat|Carbon blue credits|Dugong/manatee tourism|Coastal storm protection value
trade_directions: Long: blue carbon credits (seagrass restoration)|Short: inshore trawling-dependent operators (gear regulation tightening)
commodity_signal: Seagrass is a nursery habitat for commercial species including sea bass, mullet, and prawns. Loss is slow but directional. Blue carbon market values seagrass at $190–$500/tonne C.

name: Salt marshes to tidal flats
signal_1: Marsh edge erosion rate (drone/satellite)
signal_4: Tidal flat expansion in coastal property zones
asset_classes: Coastal P&C insurance, blue carbon credits, coastal infrastructure bonds
commodities: Nursery habitat for marine species|Blue carbon credits|Coastal flood insurance|Real estate in estuarine zones
trade_directions: Long: blue carbon restoration credits|Short: inshore seine net fisheries (habitat loss)|Short: estuarine property insurance
commodity_signal: Salt marshes store carbon at 40x the rate of tropical forests per area. Their loss accelerates coastal flooding and removes juvenile fish habitat. Blue carbon credit prices currently $15–50/t CO2e.

name: Arctic benthos borealisation
signal_1: Species range shifts northward (trawl surveys)
signal_2: New commercial species appearance in Arctic waters
signal_4: Arctic fishing rights and treaty disputes
asset_classes: Arctic fisheries licences, sovereign risk (arctic states), shipping routes
commodities: Snow crab & cold-water shrimp|Temperate species (cod moving north)|Arctic eco-tourism
trade_directions: Long: Norwegian & Icelandic cod futures (northward range expansion)|Short: Canadian snow crab quota (southern range contraction)|Long: Svalbard/Arctic tourism operators
commodity_signal: Warm-water species colonising Arctic seabed displace cold-adapted fauna. Atlantic cod range now extends into Barents Sea areas previously off-limits. Watch ICES quota decisions.

name: Primary productivity — Arctic Ocean
signal_1: MODIS/Sentinel chl-a anomalies in Arctic
asset_classes: Arctic fisheries, carbon sequestration accounting, O&G (via sea ice context)
commodities: Capelin & herring|Krill (Antarctic proxy)|Arctic cod|Shipping + fishing access
trade_directions: Long: Arctic fishing access rights|Long: krill-based omega-3 industry (productivity shifts)|Watch: CCAMLR quota decisions as proxy
commodity_signal: Ice loss is opening new productive ocean area. More light → more phytoplankton → new fish grounds. Russia and Norway jockeying for jurisdiction. Expect quota market emergence within 10 yr.

name: Freshwater eutrophication
signal_1: Cyanobacteria bloom frequency in reservoirs
horizon: 5–15 yr; can be rapid after threshold
asset_classes: Water utility bonds, lakefront RE, agri-chemical sector, bottled water
commodities: Fertiliser (nitrogen/phosphorus)|Freshwater aquaculture|Drinking water infrastructure|Algaecide chemicals|Irrigation-fed crops
trade_directions: Long: water treatment tech stocks|Short: intensive fertiliser users near vulnerable watersheds|Long: precision ag / slow-release fertiliser producers
commodity_signal: Fertiliser over-application creates dead-zone feedback. Phosphorus commodity traders should track Baltic and US Midwest algal bloom data — precedes regulatory nitrogen/P caps.

name: Submerged to floating plants
signal_1: Floating macrophyte cover in water bodies (satellite)
signal_4: Tourism/recreation value loss in affected lakes
asset_classes: Water utility bonds, hydropower assets, freshwater fisheries
commodities: Freshwater fish (lakes and slow rivers)|Water hyacinth biomass|Navigation & irrigation access
trade_directions: Long: aquatic weed management industry|Short: inland water transport in affected regions (Congo Basin, Lake Victoria)
commodity_signal: Water hyacinth on Lake Victoria is already disrupting Nile perch trade ($250M/yr). Floating plant transitions driven by nutrient loading from agriculture — phosphorus a key control variable.

name: Forest to savanna
signal_1: Amazon deforestation rate + rainfall recycling models
signal_4: Carbon credit permanence risk in forest offsets
horizon: Amazon: potentially <15 yr from tipping if deforestation continues
asset_classes: Agri-commodity futures (soy, beef), forest carbon credits, Brazilian sovereign/corporate bonds
commodities: Soy & cattle (Amazon)|Timber|Carbon credits|Brazilian sovereign bonds|Hydropower
trade_directions: Short: Amazon-adjacent hydro operators (reduced flow)|Long: carbon offset certificates (deforestation pressure)|Long: cerrado soy (less fire risk than forest-frontier)|Watch: Brazilian real exposure
commodity_signal: Amazon dieback tipping point (40% deforestation threshold) could be within 10 yr. Soy traders must model biome-level precipitation collapse, not just farm-level yield.

name: Peatland transitions
signal_1: Peat fire frequency and area burned
horizon: Decades; peat fires can be rapid
asset_classes: Carbon credits (peatland restoration), palm oil / peat-adjacent agri, re-insurance
commodities: Carbon credits (peat stores ~30% of soil carbon)|Palm oil (SE Asian peat drainage)|Scotch whisky (Scottish peatland water)|Timber (boreal peat)|Peat-based horticulture
trade_directions: Short: peat-drained palm oil concessions (regulatory risk)|Long: peat-free horticulture alternatives|Long: voluntary carbon markets (peat rewetting projects)
commodity_signal: Drained peatlands are largest per-ha carbon emitters. EU taxonomy will increasingly exclude peat-drained palm oil. CPO futures traders should price in land-use regulation premium.

name: Bush encroachment
signal_1: Woody cover expansion rate in savanna/grassland (MODIS)
signal_3: Water table drawdown in encroached areas
asset_classes: Livestock/ranching equities, agri-land values in sub-Saharan Africa, carbon removal credits
commodities: Southern African beef & game|Safari tourism|Charcoal & bush biomass|Carbon credits (bush-to-charcoal)|Land values (Namibia, Botswana, Zimbabwe)
trade_directions: Long: biomass energy (invasive bush as feedstock)|Long: charcoal export from Namibia/Botswana|Short: cattle stocking rates on encroached land|Long: thorn-resistant livestock breeds
commodity_signal: Invasive Acacia and Dichrostachys species reducing African pasture productivity by 30–60%. Namibia is monetising this as charcoal exports (€40M/yr). Carbon sequestration credits also being piloted.

name: Coniferous to deciduous forest
signal_1: Boreal tree mortality and beetle outbreak extent
signal_4: Wildfire risk premium change in conifer zones
asset_classes: Timber REITs, forest carbon credits, forest fire re-insurance
commodities: Softwood timber & pulp|Hardwood timber|Paper/packaging|Maple syrup|Certified forestry credits (FSC)
trade_directions: Short: softwood-focused Canadian & Nordic timber REITs|Long: hardwood futures (oak, beech, birch)|Long: pulp substitutes (bamboo, agricultural residue paper)
commodity_signal: Spruce and pine retreat northward. Boreal pulp supply will tighten. Hardwood value rises in temperate zones. Watch FSC certification shifts as species mix changes.

name: Tundra to boreal forest
signal_1: Shrub/tree line advance rate (satellite)
signal_4: Infrastructure damage cost in transitioning zones
asset_classes: Arctic sovereign bonds, carbon credits (boreal expansion offset claims), infrastructure in affected zones
commodities: Softwood timber (new boreal expansion)|Carbon credits (loses tundra albedo benefit)|Reindeer/caribou herding|Methane (permafrost release)
trade_directions: Long: Scandinavian & Canadian softwood futures (new productive forest area)|Short: traditional tundra-based herding operations|Long: methane-linked energy volatility plays
commodity_signal: Net carbon impact is complex — new forest sequesters carbon but loses tundra albedo. Timber industry gains new productive land in Canada/Russia over 30–50 yr horizon.

name: Steppe to tundra
signal_1: Vegetation greenness loss in Central Asian steppes
asset_classes: Agri-land values in Central Asia, sovereign risk (Kazakhstan, Mongolia), carbon accounting
commodities: Russian/Kazakh wheat & barley|Cashmere (Mongolian goat herding)|Pastoralism & livestock|Wool
trade_directions: Long: wheat futures (Central Asian steppe degradation reduces supply)|Short: cashmere (Mongolian pasture degradation already underway)|Long: synthetic fibre substitutes
commodity_signal: Mongolian dzud events (extreme freeze-thaw) are increasing in frequency. Direct cashmere supply risk. Central Asian wheat belt is already under soil degradation stress.

name: Thermokarst lakes
signal_1: Permafrost thaw lake area change (satellite)
detectability_note: satellite lake area change well documented
asset_classes: Arctic O&G infrastructure, pipeline operators, sovereign bonds (Russia, Canada), carbon accounting
commodities: Methane & GHG credits|Oil & gas infrastructure (permafrost)|Carbon offsets|Russian pipeline integrity|Boreal agriculture
trade_directions: Long: methane volatility instruments|Short: Siberian infrastructure-exposed energy companies|Long: permafrost monitoring tech
commodity_signal: Permafrost thaw is destabilising Russian oil/gas infrastructure. Gazprom and Novatek have quantified billions in maintenance uplift. Also releases legacy carbon creating SCC feedback.

name: Indian summer monsoon
signal_1: Monsoon onset timing and deficit anomalies
signal_4: South Asian sovereign credit risk premium
horizon: Non-linear risk; current weakening trend observable
asset_classes: Indian agri-commodity futures, South Asian sovereign bonds, P&C insurance books, water-intensive Indian corporates
commodities: Indian wheat, rice, sugar cane|Cotton|Hydropower (Himalayan rivers)|Food price index (FAO)|Indian rupee
trade_directions: Long: food price volatility instruments|Short: irrigation-dependent Indian ag exporters in erratic monsoon years|Long: drip irrigation & precision water tech|Watch: INR-linked agricultural commodity trades
commodity_signal: Monsoon onset timing is already shifting. 1.4B people depend on it. Rice and sugar markets react to Indian monsoon forecasts within days. El Niño interaction amplifies volatility.

name: River channel change
signal_1: Channel migration rates (satellite time-series)
asset_classes: Floodplain RE, river infrastructure bonds, flood insurance, agri-land near dynamic rivers
commodities: Freshwater fisheries|Irrigation-fed crops|Hydropower generation|Sand & gravel mining|Flood insurance
trade_directions: Long: riverine flood reinsurance|Short: sand mining licences in unstable channels|Long: adaptive irrigation infrastructure
commodity_signal: Glacier-fed rivers (Ganges, Brahmaputra, Colorado) facing flow regime change. Hydropower operators are already revising long-term generation forecasts. Sand and gravel supply disrupted by channel migration.

name: Common pool resource harvesting
signal_1: Harvest effort vs. stock ratio trends
horizon: Context-dependent; can be rapid after governance failure
asset_classes: Fisheries quota assets, forestry concessions, sovereign bonds where resource is GDP-significant
commodities: Open-access fisheries (IUU fishing)|Community forestry|Shared grazing|Groundwater
trade_directions: Long: quota-managed fisheries over open-access|Long: groundwater futures (emerging)|Watch: IUU seafood labelling regulations (EU, US)
commodity_signal: Tragedy of commons dynamics drive race-to-fish. IUU fishing represents 20% of global catch. Supply chain traceability regulation (EU deforestation law, US Seafood Import Monitoring) creates compliance cost premium.

name: Sprawling vs compact cities
signal_1: Urban land expansion rate vs. population growth ratio
asset_classes: Urban RE (spatial distribution matters), transport infrastructure, municipal bonds, energy utilities
commodities: Peri-urban agricultural land|Real estate & REITs|Transport energy|Urban food systems
trade_directions: Long: urban food tech (vertical farming, urban ag) in sprawl-constrained markets|Long: transit-oriented real estate|Short: car-dependent retail REITs
commodity_signal: Urban sprawl converts ~1.2M ha/yr of agricultural land globally. This is a slow but compounding pressure on peri-urban food supply. Most tradeable via real estate and urban infrastructure debt.
